title: Green-space and soil-sealing monitoring for liveability
summary: Land cover products track green space and soil sealing to support liveable, compliant urban planning.
problem: Urban densification erodes green space and increases soil sealing, harming liveability, biodiversity and stormwater management. Cities need consistent, repeatable monitoring to track change and demonstrate policy compliance.
solution: CLMS Urban Atlas, imperviousness and tree-cover products derived from Sentinel-2 provide harmonised, repeatable measurement of green space and sealed surfaces. These support planning decisions, green-infrastructure strategies and reporting against EU and local targets.

copernicusRole.detail: CLMS Urban Atlas and imperviousness products are used largely as delivered to measure green space and sealing, with light analysis for reporting.
